title: Manifest model and format versioning
purpose: Define the manifest — the package's self-describing deployment descriptor — as a typed struct that serialises to and from manifest.json. The manifest tells the engine the entry point, the input/output shapes, the timeout, the declared activities, the content-hash version, and the format version of the .aion layout itself.
task: Define the Manifest struct carrying entry_module, entry_function, input_schema, output_schema, timeout, declared activities, version (the content hash, as a value type), and format_version. Derive serde and verify lossless round-trip through manifest.json. Model declared activities and the JSON-Schema input/output descriptions as typed fields. Define the format_version constant and the surface that rejects an unknown value (the rejection itself fires in the read path, AP-006; this brief defines the field, the current value, and the check helper). Do not parse the archive or compute the hash here.

requirements.title: Manifest struct and serde round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define Manifest as a typed struct carrying entry_module, entry_function, input_schema, output_schema, timeout, declared activities, version, and format_version, deriving Serialize and Deserialize, round-tripping losslessly through serde_json.
acceptance: manifest.rs defines Manifest with fields: entry_module (logical module name), entry_function (String), input_schema, output_schema, timeout, activities, version, format_version.
Manifest derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, PartialEq.
A unit test serialises a Manifest to JSON and deserialises it back, asserting equality (lossless round-trip).
Field names in the JSON form are stable and documented (the on-disk manifest.json key names are part of the format).
timeout is std::time::Duration (serde-serialised), not a bare integer with an undocumented unit.

requirements.title: Schemas and declared activities
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL model input_schema and output_schema as JSON-Schema descriptions of the workflow input and result Payload shapes, and SHALL model declared activities as a list where each entry identifies its activity type.
acceptance: input_schema and output_schema hold JSON-Schema documents as serde_json::Value, describing the input and result Payload shapes (a JSON-Schema document is itself JSON, so serde_json::Value is the representation — no typed wrapper).
Declared activities are a list of entries, each carrying at least the activity type identifier the workflow invokes.
The activity-type identifiers use aion-core's conventions (no bare ad-hoc string type where aion-core provides a type).
A unit test constructs a Manifest with two declared activities and a non-trivial input/output schema and round-trips it.

requirements.title: Format version field and check
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define format_version identifying the .aion format schema version, expose the current supported value, and provide a check that returns the UnknownFormatVersion PackageError for any other value.
acceptance: A constant defines the current supported format_version, and Manifest carries the field.
A function (e.g. Manifest::check_format_version) returns Ok for the supported value and Err(PackageError::UnknownFormatVersion { found }) otherwise.
A doc comment states that format_version lets a future layout change be detected rather than silently misread.
A unit test asserts the supported value passes and an unsupported value yields UnknownFormatVersion.

boundaries: SHALL NOT open or parse the ZIP archive — that is the read path in AP-006.
SHALL NOT compute or set the content hash — AP-003 computes it; AP-005 stamps it into the version field.
SHALL NOT validate input Payloads against input_schema at runtime — schema validation is a consumer concern (engine/server), not the package format.
The UnknownFormatVersion rejection is invoked from the read path (AP-006); this brief defines the field, the constant, and the check helper only.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
